retrieved_contexts:
* Run the installer executable (SPSS_Modeler_141_win64.exe) as normal. When it finishes extracting and decompressing the .msi file and the end-user is required to click 'Continue', minimise this window. 

* Switch to the %temp% folder and locate the folder with the 'ModelerClient64.msi' file. Copy ALL contents within this folder and paste it to a local folder (e.g. C:\Temp\Modeler\ClientInstall). 

* Once copied, switch back to the installation wizard and hit 'Cancel'.

TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What causes an 1152 error during extraction of Modeler self extracting executable? 

CAUSE
When an IBM SPSS compressed image is launched, files compressed within it are extracted to the temporary location assigned to the signed in user i.e. /Documents and settings/... or /users.
The possibility of these files downloaded improperly exists. You can use a tool such
as MD5 to verify the file content or simply attempt downloading a second time and test.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
 

Having a 64-bit install of Modeler and a 32-bit installation of Statistics is not a supported configuration by IBM SPSS. In order to use the SPSS Statistics nodes from within Modeler, you will need to install the 64-bit version of Statistics. 

This is because the 32-bit API's of SPSS Statistics cannot be loaded by the 64-bit installation of SPSS Modeler. 

 

This limitation is expected to be documented in future releases of IBM SPSS Modeler.

1. Insert the disk.
2. IF prompted to install the software, select 'Cancel'.
3. Browse to the media location. 
4. Open the 'SPSSStatistics' folder.

ANSWER

Using the Software Download:

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Configure the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v7r0/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.websphere.nd.multiplatform.doc/info/ae/ae/udat_conpoolset.html] so as to eliminate occurrences of StaleConnectionException :

Stale Connection Exception; StaleConnectionException; Websphere JCC driver -4499 db2/z zOS; 0x2413; 0x2408 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 
Websphere Application aborts after calling z/OS stored procedure because of stale connection exceptions. 

SYMPTOM
StaleConnectionException may be seen after upgrade JCC driver. 

Websphere log file:

If the J2CA0036E and DSRA0023E are logged in the application server's SystemOut.log, then check the JDBC Provider configuration to see if it uses WebSphere variables for its classpath setting. From the Administrative Console select Resources > JDBC Provider > DB_Provider_Used_By_DataSource, and inspect the classpath. For this example, one would check to see if the JDBC Provider configuration uses the Application Server's DB2_INSTALL_ROOT and DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH variable.

ENVIRONMENT
The exception com.ibm.websphere.ce.cm.StaleConnectionException is specific to WebSphere Application Server environments. But a similar issue can be experienced with Weblogic, see: Rule Execution Server on WebLogic unable to load ruleset after period of inactivity [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21615801]
per_question.response: To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, configure the data source connection pool to eliminate occurrences of this exception. Additionally, ensure that the JDBC Provider configuration uses WebSphere variables for its classpath setting, specifically checking the DB2_INSTALL_ROOT and DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH variables.
per_question.reference:
To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, you need to set the "Validate on Reserve" property to true for the connection pool associated with the data source in WebSphere Application Server. This setting will ensure that the connections are validated before being returned to the application to prevent stale connections. 

Additionally, you may need to adjust the maximum idle time setting in the Oracle database to prevent the ORA-02396 error. This can be done by modifying the SQLNET.ORA file on the Oracle server to increase the value of the SQLNET.EXPIRE_TIME parameter. 

By setting "Validate on Reserve" to true in the connection pool and adjusting the maximum idle time in the Oracle database, you should be able to resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server.

How can I export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance? 

How can I export a private key from DataPower Gateway appliance?
retrieved_contexts:
TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I export and import private keys between the same or different Hardware Security Module (HSM) enabled IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance? 

ANSWER
HSM-enabled DataPower appliances support the export of private keys using the crypto-export command. For key export to work, various conditions must be met:

If the CA in question is needed by the customer, then the user must obtain an updated copy from the CA's public site, upload the new certificate to the DataPower appliance, and then reconfigure the down Crypto Certificate object to refer to the new certificate file.

ANSWER
To add the Data Integration Module: 

 * The appliance must be a DataPower Service Gateway XG45 running firmware 4.0.2.0 or later. 
 * You purchased the Data Integration Module and received a letter that contains the Controlled Access Key to download the firmware image.

First, know your certificate material. It's not uncommon for newer certificates to be chained, so it is not a one for one replacement; the existing certificate on the DataPower appliance may need to be replaced by more than one certificate. Therefore, it is important to ensure you have the full certificate chain, as appropriate (i.e, the personal, intermediate and root certificates)

Once all of this has been done, then private keys may move from system to system with crypto-export and crypto-import. 

Note that the non-HSM appliance can export keys immediately at keygen time, but never at a later time. 

For additional information refer to the online Info Center [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v3r8m2/index.jsp?topic=%2Fxi50%2Fwelcome.htm] and search for "PIN entry device".
per_question.response: To export a private key from a DataPower Gateway appliance, you can use the crypto-export command, ensuring that all necessary conditions are met. Additionally, if the certificate authority (CA) is required, you must obtain an updated copy of the CA's certificate, upload it to the appliance, and reconfigure the Crypto Certificate object accordingly.
per_question.reference:
To export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance, you can use the `crypto-export` command. However, to successfully export a private key, the following conditions must be met:

1. Ensure that the HSMs on both the exporting and importing machines are initialized and in the same key sharing domain.
2. The private key you want to export must be marked as exportable at the time of key generation.
3. The HSMs on both machines must share internal key-wrapping keys. These keys encrypt the exported private keys and decrypt imported private keys.
4. If you are moving keys from one appliance to another, synchronize the key-wrapping keys using the `hsm-clone-kwk` command on both appliances.

Follow these steps to successfully export a private key from a DataPower Gateway Appliance using the `crypto-export` command.

There are three use cases to configure Sharing Conversations in DataPower mq-qm object: 

Case #1 : The negotiated sharing conversations value is 0 - The channel runs in a mode similar to WebSphere MQ V6 and does not uses features such as

· Administrator stop-quiesce
· Heartbeating 
· Read ahead 
· Client asynchronous consume

Since DataPower uses a connection pool in processing MQ messages, there are no additional benefits of using sharing conversations with the mq-qm object. However, in situations when mixed message sizes are used by the same mq-qm object, using case #2 will benefit mq-qm object as it will use new buffer management feature of IBM WebSphere MQ Version 7 when making MQGET API call.

Cases #2 and #3 can be used in firmware releases 7.5.0.x, 7.5.1.x and later. We suggest to use a value of 10 or less in Sharing Conversations attribute of the mq-qm object and verify the configuration empirically to make sure that IBM WebSphere MQ server can sustain load from DataPower with the negotiated Sharing Conversations of 10.

For DataPower firmware releases 7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x and earlier, one should follow case #1 and use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0. If you configure these firmware versions with case #2 or case #3, DataPower device may reload or hang due to connection instability and generate many MQ errors with Reason Code - 2009 and 2059.

Set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower as shown in the following picture and a value of 1 on IBM WebSphere MQ SVRCONN setting.
per_question.response: You should use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers when using firmware releases 7.5.0.x, 7.5.1.x, and later, and it is recommended to set a value of 10 or less in the Sharing Conversations attribute. For earlier firmware releases (7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x), you should follow case #1 and use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0 to avoid connection instability and errors.
per_question.reference:
Conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers should be used when negotiating the sharing conversations value. It is beneficial to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute with a specific value based on the negotiated value with the MQ server. 

Three different use cases for configuring sharing conversations in DataPower MQ queue managers are outlined:
1. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 0, it is recommended to set a value of 0 or 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute in DataPower to disable sharing conversations.
2. If the negotiated sharing conversations value is 1, it is advised to set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute in DataPower. 
3. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 2 or more, it is recommended to set a value of 2 or more on the Sharing Conversations attribute.

It is important to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute appropriately to ensure optimal performance and avoid potential errors or instability in the MQ environment.

retrieved_contexts:

 Unable to view images in reports in PDF/Excel output when using Report Studio/Workspace.

Unable to view images in reports in PDF/Excel output when using Report Studio/Workspace. 
However images are visible in HTML format when reports are run actively.

secure socket layer IR440015 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 In a multi-tier environment with SSL enabled on the webserver, images are not being displayed when report run as PDF. Images are being shown as just a blank box in PDF. Running the report in HTML displays the images. 

CAUSE
Incomplete or missing Chain of Trust for SSL within the IBM Cognos Keystore

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
There are two options available:

The PDF/Excel outputs will have a placeholder image. This will be in the form of an empty black box (PDF) or will be a missing pictures icon (Excel).


CAUSE
There are a few common causes for this issue: 

 1. The permissions of the folder which contains the image, have not been configured correctly 
 2. The image is not within the directory which is specified 
 3. The directory where the image is meant to be stored, does not exist in the specified location

1. Open up IIS 7.5 
 2. Click on the root folder of your Cognos installation (E.g. C1021GA) in the navigation pane on the left side 
 3. When the root folder is selected, double-click 'Authentication' 
 4. Ensure that anonymous access is enabled 
 5. Repeat steps 3 and 4 for the image folder and it's parent folder. 
 6. If the user is concerned about security, they may want to restrict the child-folders (E.g. cgi-bin) and change the authentication settings accordingly

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
There are two options available: 

 1. Place the images on the application tier in the Cognos installation directory in the same path they exist on the gateway server. This way the Application Tier can access the image on disk, removing the need to connect to HTTPS
    
    Example: <cognos install>/webcontent/samples/images/pic.jpg 
 2.  
 3. Import the SSL certificate Chain of Trust on the Application Tier.

per_question.reference:

To troubleshoot and potentially resolve the issue, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the permissions of the folder containing the images to ensure they are configured correctly.

2. Verify that the images are located within the directory specified in the report.

3. If the image directory is not found, ensure that the images are placed in the specified location.

4. If SSL is enabled and causing issues with image display in PDF, ensure that the SSL certificate Chain of Trust is imported on the Application Tier.

5. Import the CA certificate and any Intermediate certificates in the chain of trust in the IBM Cognos CA keystore using the ThirdPartyCertificateTool.

By following these steps, you may be able to resolve the issue of images not being displayed in PDF/Excel reports in Cognos Analytics 11.0.3.

per_question.reference: The file db2jcc.jar is missing after an upgrade to BPM V8.5.7 CF2016.12 because IBM BPM does not transform complex IBM BPM types (such as TWObjects) to standard JDBC types that the database can process. Therefore, the SQL Connector code can only handle simple types. In the meantime, a temporary fix is to use the Java integration feature to invoke custom Java code using JDBC java interfaces to call stored procedures.

retrieved_contexts:
WebSphere Application Server V8.0, V8.5, V8.5.5
Starting with V8.0 and applicable to all subsequent releases and fixpacks, there is one install package that contains updates for both WebSphere Application Server and Java SDK. Applying fixpacks of WebSphere Application Server also upgrades the SDK that is packaged and bundled within the fixpack. Additionally, separate Java SDK fixpacks are also available that contain just the SDK updates which can be applied to upgrade the SDK.

ABSTRACT
 IBM Ask the Experts discussion about WebSphere Application Server installation/uninstall/update/modify using Install Manager on 12 December 2013. 
This session covers install / uninstall / update / modify operations using Installation Manager on WebSphere Application Server V8.X This also covers any questions related to Install /Uninstall or applying fixpacks using Update Installer in v7.x

Ask the Experts sessions are conducted in a question and answer format.

of WebSphere Application Server through Installation Mananger? 23:01 When we go for Ifix and when we go for fixpack, what is basic difference between those? 23:50 Can you pleasee give information about what is the use of Liberty profile and its advantages? 24:57 What do you recommend if there is a need to duplicate the WebSphere environment? 26:54 What are the basic pre-req checks for installation of Installation Manager? 28:19 Do you have a link for how to federate the 64-bit WAS ND APPSERVER

WebSphere Application Server V7.0
Any V7.0 fix pack can upgrade to the latest Java SDK 1.6.0 service release packaged for WebSphere Application Server.

WebSphere Application Server V6.1
Any V6.1 fix pack can upgrade to the latest Java SDK 1.5.0 service release packaged for WebSphere Application Server.

WebSphere Application Server V6.0

Once this is done, you perform these tasks:  * Repackage your client applications with the up-to-date libraries.

per_question.response: This change occurred starting with WebSphere Application Server V8.0, where a single install package was introduced that contains updates for both WebSphere Application Server and the Java SDK. Prior to this version, separate fixpacks were used for WebSphere and the JDK.
per_question.reference: The change to using Installation Manager to install fixpacks for both WebSphere Application Server and the Java SDK occurred with WebSphere Application Server V8.0. During startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store I see hanging threads in the systemOut.log and the process will not finish. How can this be solved?
retrieved_contexts:
*

The I/O would stop in a storage failure due to the settings, then it causes a hang issue on pureScale cluster. Please see the Linux manual page for more information on the queue_if_no_path settings.

SYMPTOM
DB transactions are rolled back due to SQL timeout.


CAUSE
When multiple DB2 pureScale members are active during FileNet CM addon creation, excessive SQL locking may occur, resulting in a potential lock timeout. This can result in transactions being rolled back, thereby resulting in the addon installation failing.

DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM
Examine both the P8 error log and the DB2 diagnostic logs for each active member for lock timeout error messages similar to the following:

at com.ibm.wps.engine.phases.WPActionPhase
   .execute(WPActionPhase.java:668)
   at com.ibm.wps.state.phases.AbstractActionPhase
   .next(AbstractActionPhase.java:130)
   at com.ibm.wps.engine.Servlet.callPortal(Servlet.java:855)
   at com.ibm.wps.engine.Servlet.doGet(Servlet.java:617)
   at com.ibm.wps.engine.Servlet.doPost(Servlet.java:888)
   ...
   
   Some minutes later the Application Server will report hang
   threads:

As well DB2 has it's own set of traces that will allow you to catch the files
opened and closed by the DB2 internal API. Note that this might not be as complete
as tracing the regular 'open()' and 'close()' system calls at the process level.
For example you can do the following:

# db2trc on -f trc.raw -Madd sqloopenp -Madd sqloclose


- 6 - Extra notes.

Note: The SQLERRMC=68 suggests that the root cause is a SQL lock timeout even though the P8 error message and SQLCODE suggest a deadlock occurred.


RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
The lock timeouts can be avoided by only having a single DB2 pureScale member active during FileNet CM addon installation. Once addon installation has completed successfully, the other members can be brought back online.
per_question.reference:
Yes, the BPM internal Document Store can work with IBM DB2 pureScale. If you are experiencing hanging threads during startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store, it may be due to issues with the pureScale setup and configuration. 

One potential solution is to ensure that the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity are correctly configured on Tomcat. This involves setting pureScale member host names in the operating system's hosts file, adding JNDI resource information to the web application environment file, adding JNDI data source information to the Tomcat configuration file, and implementing custom code to retrieve supported user attributes from the underlying security repository.

By correctly configuring the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity on Tomcat, you may be able to resolve the hanging threads issue during BPM server startup and internal document store initialization.

per_question.response: Yes, the java.io.NotSerializableException is a known issue for WebSphere Application Server version 6 users, where it is repeatedly logged when persistent sessions or PMI is enabled. The notification code attempts to resend messages upon encountering this exception, leading to multiple entries in the server log. A fix is targeted for inclusion in specific fixpacks.
per_question.reference:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.

long as the thread is detected as hung, using the
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track property.
   If this property is unset, the default value is set to be
   identical to the value of
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.  If the property is
   set to "true", the thread monitor sets those properties to the
   maximum integer value, so a thread hung for a long period of
   time would cause javacores to be thrown basically

There are 3 options to correct this:

1) Try the classification "add attribute" function and consider increasing JVM memory if needed. This is the officially supported way to handle your use case. Note, delete the attribute before trying to add it again.

2) Delete all of your authorized CIs and then promote again. Do not use 
the CI Space Loader tool to load new attributes and you will not have 
this issue again.

zFS files not propagated to target environment(s)
Please see your application server documentation for how to disable this capability.
per_question.reference:
Based on the provided context, the issue with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state could be due to the zFS files not being properly propagated to the target environments after applying preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS. When the updated zFS files are not rolled out to the target environments, it can impact the functioning of the JVM Servers.

To resolve this issue, ensure that the updated zFS files are properly propagated to all target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS. This should allow the JVMSERVER to be disabled successfully without getting stuck in a "BEING DISABLED" state.

per_question.user_input: Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method? Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests?
retrieved_contexts:
Users configuring DataPower may need a way to implement this CONNECT method for SSL tunneling to a remote Proxy Server.




RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Non-SSL Proxy requests will work using either:

a) an XML Firewall when configuring in the HTTP Options tab, where the user can configure the Proxy Host and Port

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I add the Data Integration Module to an IBM WebSphere DataPower Service Gateway XG45 appliance?

The following document provides assistance on upgrading a DataPower Service Gateway XG45 appliance to add the Data Integration Module. 

ANSWER
To add the Data Integration Module:

ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Datapower service (i.e MPGW, WSP, etc) is configured to connect
   to backend server through an HTTP Proxy server (as configured
   in the Proxy Policy of the user agent) using the HTTP CONNECT
   method.
   However, if the HTTP CONNECT request to the proxy server,
   receives a 5xx response, Datapower might issue the following SSL

 How to configure an IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance Service to proxy an SSL request to a remote proxy server using the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling? 

CAUSE

per_question.reference: Yes, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance does support the HTTP CONNECT method. QUESTION
 What are the best practices for deploying Datacap servers and clients on a wide area network (WAN)? 

ANSWER
Remote users that access Datacap over a WAN can use Taskmaster Web-based "thin clients", or FastDoc Capture operating in offline mode. Datacap thick clients (DotScan, DotEdit) and utilities (NENU, Fingerprint Maintenance Tool) require LAN communication speeds and low latency for responsive performance.
